metadata.title: NCA Cybersecurity Frameworks – OSCAL Catalog
metadata.remarks: Machine-readable OSCAL representation of all NCA cybersecurity frameworks: ECC, CSCC, CCC, DCC, TCC, OSMACC, and NCS. Generated automatically from structured NCA source data. Issuer: National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA), Saudi Arabia.

props.name: description
props.value: Foundational cybersecurity controls required for all organisations operating in Saudi Arabia.

controls.title: The cybersecurity strategy of the entity shall be identified, documented, and ap

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity strategy of the entity shall be identified, documented, and approved, and it shall be supported by the head of the entity or his/her delegate (Hereinafter referred to as the “Authorized Official”). The strategy goals shall be in line with the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the action plans, objectives, initiatives, and projects of the entity contribute to compliance with the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: A department for cybersecurity shall be established within the entity. This depa

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A department for cybersecurity shall be established within the entity. This department shall be independent from the Information Technology and Communications Department (As per High Order No. 37140, dated 14/08/1438H.). It is recommended that the Cybersecurity Department reports directly to the head of the entity or his/her delegate while ensuring that this does not result in a conflict of interests.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the Authorized Official of the entity complies with and supports the implementation and management of cybersecurity programs within the entity, as per the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: All cybersecurity positions shall be filled out with full-time and qualified Sau

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All cybersecurity positions shall be filled out with full-time and qualified Saudi cybersecurity professionals.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the Authorized Official of the entity complies with and supports the implementation and management of cybersecurity programs within the entity, as per the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: A cybersecurity supervisory committee shall be established pursuant to the instr

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A cybersecurity supervisory committee shall be established pursuant to the instruction of the entity’s Authorized Official to ensure compliance with, support for, and monitoring of the implementation of the cybersecurity programs and regulations. The committee’s members, responsibilities, and governance framework shall be identified, documented, and approved. The committee shall include the head of the cybersecurity department as a member. It is recommended that the committee reports directly to the head of the entity or his/her delegate while ensuring that this does not result in a conflict of interests.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the Authorized Official of the entity complies with and supports the implementation and management of cybersecurity programs within the entity, as per the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity department of the entity shall identify and document cybersecurity policies and procedures, including the cybersecurity controls and requirements, and have them approved by the entity’s Authorized Official, and communicate them to the relevant personnel and parties inside the entity.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the cybersecurity requirements and the entity’s compliance therewith are documented and communicated, as per the entity’s regulatory requirements and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective

groups.title: Cybersecurity in Information and Technology Project Management

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements shall be included in the project management methodolo

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements shall be included in the project management methodology and procedures and in the information and technology asset change management within the entity to ensure identifying and managing cybersecurity risks as part of the technology project lifecycle. The cybersecurity requirements shall be a key part of the requirements for technology projects.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in the methodology and procedures of the entity’s project management, in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity, accuracy, and availability of the entity’s information and technology assets, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: The cybersecurity requirements for project management and information and techno

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity requirements for project management and information and technology asset changes within the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.2.1 Vulnerability assessment and remediation.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.2.2 Reviewing secure configuration and hardening and updates packages before launching projects and changes.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in the methodology and procedures of the entity’s project management, in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity, accuracy, and availability of the entity’s information and technology assets, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: The cybersecurity requirements for software and application development projects

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity requirements for software and application development projects within the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.3.1 Using the secure coding standards.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.3.2 Using trusted and licensed sources for software development tools and libraries.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.3.3 Conducting compliance test for software against the cybersecurity requirements within the entity.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.3.4 Secure integration between applications.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.6.3.5 Reviewing secure configuration and hardening and updates packages before launching software products

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in the methodology and procedures of the entity’s project management, in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity, accuracy, and availability of the entity’s information and technology assets, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.prose: To ensure that the cybersecurity controls adopted by the entity are implemented and applicable in accordance with the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures, relevant national legislative and regulatory requirements, and international requirements imposed on the entity by law.

controls.title: The implementation of cybersecurity controls by the entity shall be reviewed and

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The implementation of cybersecurity controls by the entity shall be reviewed and audited by parties other than the cybersecurity department at the entity, provided that the audit and review are to be conducted independently while considering the principle of conflict of interest, as per the Generally Accepted Auditing Standards (GAAS) and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the cybersecurity controls adopted by the entity are implemented and applicable in accordance with the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures, relevant national legislative and regulatory requirements, and international requirements imposed on the entity by law.

parts.name: statement

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements for personnel (employees and contractors) of the entity are managed efficiently prior to, during, and upon the end or termination of their employment, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements prior to the commencement of the employment relations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements prior to the commencement of the employment relationship between personnel and the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.9.3.1 Incorporating the personnel’s cybersecurity responsibilities clauses and non-disclosure clauses in their employment contracts with the entity (including during and after employment end/termination with the entity).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.9.3.2 Conducting screening or vetting for personnel in cybersecurity positions and technical positions with critical and privileged powers.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements for personnel (employees and contractors) of the entity are managed efficiently prior to, during, and upon the end or termination of their employment, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for personnel during their employment relationship with the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.9.4.1 Cybersecurity awareness (during on-boarding and during employment).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.9.4.2 Implementation and compliance with cybersecurity requirements, as per the entity’s cybersecurity policies, procedures, and operations.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements for personnel (employees and contractors) of the entity are managed efficiently prior to, during, and upon the end or termination of their employment, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures and the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

controls.title: A cybersecurity awareness program, delivered through multiple channels, shall be

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A cybersecurity awareness program, delivered through multiple channels, shall be periodically developed and approved by the entity to strengthen the awareness about cybersecurity, cyber threats, and risks, and to build a positive cybersecurity awareness culture.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the entity’s personnel have the required security awareness, are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities, and are equipped with the required cybersecurity skills, qualifications, and training courses in order to protect the entity’s information and technology assets and fulfill their cybersecurity duties.

controls.title: The cybersecurity awareness program shall include how to protect the entity agai

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity awareness program shall include how to protect the entity against the most important and latest cyber risks and threats, including:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.3.1 Secure handling of email services, especially phishing emails.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.3.2 Secure handling of mobile devices and storage media.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.3.3 Secure Internet browsing.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.3.4 Secure usage of social media.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the entity’s personnel have the required security awareness, are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities, and are equipped with the required cybersecurity skills, qualifications, and training courses in order to protect the entity’s information and technology assets and fulfill their cybersecurity duties.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.4.1 Cybersecurity department personnel.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.4.2 Personnel working on software/application development and those working on information and technology assets of the entity.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1.10.4.3 Executive and supervisory positions.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the entity’s personnel have the required security awareness, are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities, and are equipped with the required cybersecurity skills, qualifications, and training courses in order to protect the entity’s information and technology assets and fulfill their cybersecurity duties.

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management of the entity shal

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management of the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.2.3.1 Single-factor authentication based on username and password.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.2.3.2 Multi-factor authentication, and defining the suitable authentication factors and their numbers as well as the suitable authentication techniques based on the result of impact assessment of authentication failure and bypass for remote access and for privileged accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.2.3.3 User authorization based on identity and access control principles (Need-to-Know and Need-to-Use principle, Least Privilege principle, and Segregation of Duties principle).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.2.3.4 Privileged access management.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.2.3.5 Periodic review of identities and access rights.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure protecting cybersecurity of logical access to information and technology assets of the entity, in order to prevent unauthorized access and restrict access to the extent necessary for accomplishment of the assigned tasks of the entity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for protection of information systems and processing facilities of the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.3.3.1 Protection from viruses, suspicious programs and activities, and malware on workstations and servers, using modern and advanced protection technologies and mechanisms, and securely managing them.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.3.3.2 Strict restriction on the use of external storage media and their security.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.3.3.3 Patch management for systems, applications, and devices.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.3.3.4 Centralized clock synchronization with an accurate and trusted source, such as sources provided by the Saudi Standards, Metrology and Quality Organization (SASO).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of information systems and processing facilities, including workstations and infrastructures of the entity, against cyber risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for protection of the email service of the entity shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.4.3.1 Analyzing and filtering email messages (specifically phishing emails and spam emails) using modern and advanced email protection techniques and mechanisms.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.4.3.2 Multi-factor authentication, and defining the suitable authentication factors and their numbers as well as the suitable authentication techniques based on the result of impact assessment of authentication failure and bypass for remote and webmail access.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.4.3.3 Email archiving and backup.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.4.3.4 Secure management and protection against Advanced Persistent Threats (APT), which normally utilize zero-day malware and viruses.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.4.3.5 Validation of the entity’s email service domains by using Sender Policy Framework (SPF), Domain Keys Identified Mail (DKIM), and Domain Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance (DMARC).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the entity’s email service against cyber risks.

parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for the entity’s network security management shall be implemented.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of entity’s networks against cyber risks.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for the entity’s network security management shall in

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for the entity’s network security management shall include the following as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.1 Logical or physical isolation and segmentation of network segments in a secure manner which is required to control relevant cybersecurity risks, using firewall and defense-in-depth principle.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.2 Isolation of production network from testing and development environment networks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.3 Secure browsing and internet connectivity, including strict restrictions on suspicious websites, file storage/sharing websites, and remote access websites.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.4 Wireless network security and protection using secure authentication and encryption techniques and avoiding the connection of wireless networks to the entity’s internal network, except after a comprehensive assessment of subsequent risks, with handling them in a way that protects the technology assets of the entity.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.5 Restricting and managing network services, protocols, and ports.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.6 Intrusion Prevention Systems (IPS).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.7 Security of Domain Name Service (DNS).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2.5.3.8 Secure management and protection of Internet browsing channel against Advanced Persistent Threats (APT), which normally utilize zero-day malware and viruses.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-3-9 Protecting against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks to limit risks arising from these attacks.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of entity’s networks against cyber risks.

controls.title: The implementation of cybersecurity requirements for the entity’s network securi

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The implementation of cybersecurity requirements for the entity’s network security management shall be periodically reviewed.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of entity’s networks against cyber risks.
