parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for cloud computing and hosting services shall be periodically reviewed.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure proper and efficient remediation of cyber risks and implementation of cybersecurity requirements for cloud computing and hosting, as per the entity’s regulatory policies and procedures, relevant legislative and regulatory requirements, orders, and decisions, and to ensure the protection of the entity’s information and technology assets on cloud computing services hosted, processed, or managed by third parties.

props.name: description
props.value: Additional controls for organisations operating critical systems, extending ECC.

controls.title: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 1-1, the organization’s cybersecuri

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 1-1, the organization’s cybersecurity strategy must prioritize the support of protecting its critical systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity plans, goals, initiatives and projects are contributing to compliance with related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 1-5, cybersecurity risk management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 1-5, cybersecurity risk management methodology must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-1-1 Conducting a cybersecurity risk assessment on critical systems at least once annually.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-1-2 Creating a cybersecurity risk register for critical systems, and reviewing it at least once every month.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure managing cybersecurity risks in a methodological approach in order to protect the organization’s information technology assets as per the organizational policies and procedures and related laws and regulations.

groups.title: Cybersecurity in Information Technology Project Management

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-6-2, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-6-2, cybersecurity requirements of project management and asset (information technology) change management of the organization’s critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-1 Conducting a stress test to ensure the capacity of the various components.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-2 Ensuring the implementation of business continuity requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in project management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information technology assets as per organization policies, and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-6-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-6-3, cybersecurity requirements related to software and application development projects of the organization’s critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-2-1 Conducting a security source code review before the critical system release.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-2-2 Securing the access, storage, documentation and releases of source code.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-2-3 Securing the authenticated Application Programming Interface (API).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-2-4 Secure and trusted migration of applications from testing environments to production environments, along with deletion of any data, IDs or passwords related to the testing environment before the migration.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in project management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information technology assets as per organization policies, and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: With reference to ECC control 1-8-1, the organization’s cybersecurity function m

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC control 1-8-1, the organization’s cybersecurity function must review the implementation of CSCC at least once annually.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity controls are implemented and in compliance with organizational policies and procedures, as well as related national and international laws, regulations and agreements.

controls.title: With reference to ECC control 1-8-2, the implementation of CSCC must be reviewed

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC control 1-8-2, the implementation of CSCC must be reviewed by independent parties within the organization, outside the cybersecurity function at least once every three years.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity controls are implemented and in compliance with organizational policies and procedures, as well as related national and international laws, regulations and agreements.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-9-3, personnel cybersecurity req

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 1-9-3, personnel cybersecurity requirements prior to employment must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-5-1-1 Screening or vetting candidates for working on critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-5-1-2 The technical support and development positions for critical systems, must be filled with experienced Saudi professionals.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements related to personnel (employees and contractors) are managed efficiently prior to employment, during employment and after termination/separation as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 2-1, cybersecurity requirements for

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 2-1, cybersecurity requirements for managing information technology assets must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-1-1 Maintaining an annually-updated inventory of critical systems’ assets.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-1-2 Identifying assets owners and involving them in the asset management lifecycle for critical systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the organization has an accurate and detailed inventory of information technology assets in order to support the organization’s cybersecurity and operational requirements to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information technical assets.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-2-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-2-3, cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management of critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-1 Prohibiting remote access from outside the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-2 Restricting remote access from inside the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and verifying each access attempt by the organization’s security operations center, and continuously monitoring activities related to remote access.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-3 Using multi-factor authentication for all users.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-4 Using multi-factor authentication for privileged users, and on systems utilized for managing critical systems stated in control 2-3-1-4.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-5 Developing and implementing a high-standard and secure password policy.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-6 Utilizing secure methods and algorithms for storing and processing passwords, such as: Hashing functions.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-7 Securely managing service accounts for applications and systems, and disabling interactive login from these accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-8 Prohibiting direct access and interaction with databases for all users except for database administrators. Users' access and interaction with databases must be through applications only, with consideration given to applying security solutions that limit or prohibit visibility of classified data to database administrators.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user identities and access rights to c

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user identities and access rights to critical systems must be reviewed at least once every three months.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

groups.title: Information System and Information Processing Facilities Protection

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-3-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-3-3, cybersecurity requirements for protecting critical systems and information processing facilities must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-1 Whitelisting of application and software operation files that are allowed to execute on servers hosting critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-2 Protecting servers hosting critical systems using end-point protection solutions that are approved by the organization.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-3 Applying security patches and updates at least once every month for external and internet-connected critical systems and at least once every three months for internal critical systems, in line with the organization’s approved change managmenet mechanisms.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-4 Allocating specific workstations in an isolated network (Management Network), that is isolated from other networks or services (e.g., email service or internet), to be used by highly privileged accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-5 Encrypting the network traffic of non-console administrative access for all technical components of critical systems using secure encryption algorithms and protocols.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-6 Reviewing critical systems’ configurations and hardening at least once every six months.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-7 Reviewing and changing default configurations, and ensuring the removal of hard-coded, backdoor and/or default passwords, where applicable.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-8 Protecting systems’ logs and critical files from unauthorized access, tampering, illegitimate modification and/or deletion.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of information systems and information processing facilities, (including workstations and infrastructures) against cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-5-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-5-3, cybersecurity requirements of critical systems’ network security management must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-1 Logically and/or physically segregating and isolating critical systems' networks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-2 Reviewing firewall rules and access lists, at least once every six months.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-3 Prohibiting direct connection between local network devices and critical systems, unless those devices are scanned to ensure they have security controls that meet the acceptable security levels for critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-4 Prohibiting critical systems from connecting to a wireless network.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-5 Protecting against Advanced Persistent Threats (APT) at the network layer.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-6 Prohibiting connection to the internet for critical systems that provide internal services to the organization and have no strong need to be accessed from outside the organization.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-7 Critical systems that provide services to a limited number of organizations (not individuals), shall use networks isolated from the Internet.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-8 Protecting against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks to limit risks arising from these attacks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-9 Allowing only whitelisting for critical systems’ firewall access lists.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the organization’s network from cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-6-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-6-3, cybersecurity requirements for mobile devices security and BYOD in the organization must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-1-1 Prohibting access to critical systems from mobile devices except for a temporary period only, after assessing the risks and obtaining the necessary approvals from the cybersecurity function in the organization.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-1-2 Implementing full disk encryption for mobile devices with access to critical systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of mobile devices (including laptops, smartphones, tablets) from cyber risks and to ensure the secure handling of the organization’s information (including sensitive information) while utilizing Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policy.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-7-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-7-3, cybersecurity requirements for protecting and handling data and information must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-1 Prohibting the use of critical systems’ data in any environment other than production environment, except after applying strict controls for protecting that data, such as: data masking or data scrambling techniques.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-2 Classifying all data within critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-3 Protecting classified data of critical systems using data leakage prevention techniques.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-4 Identifying retention period for critical systems-associated data, in accordance with relevant legislations. Only required data must be retained in critical systems’ production environments.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-5 Prohibting the transfer of any critical systems’ data from production environment to any other environment.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To Ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the organization’s data and information as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-8-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-8-3, cybersecurity requirements for cryptography must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-1-1 Encrypting all critical systems’ data-in-transit.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-1-2 Encrypting all critical systems’ data-at-rest at the level of files, database or certain columns within database.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-1-3 Using secure and up-to-date methods, algorithms, keys and devices in accordance with what NCA issues in this regard.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the proper and efficient use of cryptography to protect information assets as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-9-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-9-3, cybersecurity requirements for backup and recovery management must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-1-1 Scope and coverage of online and offline backups shall cover all critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-1-2 Performing backup within planned intervals, according to the organization’s risk assessment. NCA recommends performing backup for critical systems on a daily basis.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-1-3 Securing access, storage and transfer of critical systems’ backups and storage media, and protecting it from destruction, unauthorized access or modification.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the organization’s data and information, including information systems and software configurations from cyber risks as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-9-3-3, a periodical test must be conducted at

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-9-3-3, a periodical test must be conducted at least once every three months in order to determine the efficiency of recovering critical systems backups.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the organization’s data and information, including information systems and software configurations from cyber risks as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-10-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-10-3, cybersecurity requirements for technical vulnerabilities management of critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-1-1 Utilizing trusted methods and tools for vulnerabilities assessments.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-1-2 Assessing and remediating vulnerabilities (by installing security updates and patches) on technical components of critical systems at least once every month for external and internet-connected critical systems, and at least once every three months for internal critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-1-3 Immediately remediating for critical vulnerabilities, in line with change management processes approved by the organization.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely detection and effective remediation of technical vulnerabilities to prevent or minimize the probability of exploiting these vulnerabilities to launch cyber attacks against the organization.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-10-3-1, vulnerabilities assessments must be c

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-10-3-1, vulnerabilities assessments must be conducted on critical systems’ technical components at least once every month.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely detection and effective remediation of technical vulnerabilities to prevent or minimize the probability of exploiting these vulnerabilities to launch cyber attacks against the organization.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-11-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-11-3, cybersecurity requirements for penetration testing on critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-10-1-1 Scope of penetration tests must cover all of the critical systems’ technical components and all its internal and external services.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-10-1-2 Conducting penetration tests by a qualified team.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To assess and evaluate the efficiency of the organization’s cybersecurity defense capabilities through simulated cyber attacks to discover unknown weaknesses within the technical infrastructure that may lead to a cyber breach.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-11-3-2, penetration tests must be conducted o

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-11-3-2, penetration tests must be conducted on critical systems at least once every six months.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To assess and evaluate the efficiency of the organization’s cybersecurity defense capabilities through simulated cyber attacks to discover unknown weaknesses within the technical infrastructure that may lead to a cyber breach.

groups.title: Cybersecurity Event Logs and Monitoring Management

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirements for event logs and monitoring management for critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-1 Activating cybersecurity event logs on all technical components of critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-2 Activating and monitoring of alerts and event logs related to file integrity management.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-3 Monitoring and analyzing user behavior.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-4 Monitoring critical systems security events around the clock.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-5 Maintaining and protecting critical systems security events logs. The log shall include all details (e.g., time, date, ID and affected system).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely collection, analysis and monitoring of cybersecurity events for early detection of potential cyber-attacks in order to prevent or minimize the negative impacts on the organization’s operations.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-12-3-5, retention period of cybersecurity’s c

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-12-3-5, retention period of cybersecurity’s critical systems event logs must be 18 months minimum, in accordance with relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely collection, analysis and monitoring of cybersecurity events for early detection of potential cyber-attacks in order to prevent or minimize the negative impacts on the organization’s operations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-15-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-15-3, cybersecurity requirements for external web applications for the organization’s critical systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-1-1 Secure session management, including session authenticity, session lockout and session timeout.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-1-2 Applying the minimum standards of Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP) Top Ten.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of Internet-Facing web applications against cyber risk.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-15-3-2, multi-tier architecture principle, wi

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-15-3-2, multi-tier architecture principle, with minimum 3 tiers, must be used.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of Internet-Facing web applications against cyber risk.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must be d

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must be defined, documented, and approved.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the critical systems’ internal applications against cyber risks.

controls.title: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must be implemented.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the critical systems’ internal applications against cyber risks.

controls.title: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-13-3-1 Adopting multi-tier architecture principle, provided that number of tiers is not less than three.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-13-3-2 Using secure protocols (e.g., HTTPS).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-13-3-3 Outlining the acceptable use policy for users.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-13-3-4 Secure session management, including session authenticity, session lockout and session timeout.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the critical systems’ internal applications against cyber risks.

controls.title: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cybersecurity requirements for critical systems’ internal applications must be reviewed periodically.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of the critical systems’ internal applications against cyber risks.

groups.title: Cybersecurity Resilience Aspects of Business Continuity Management (BCM)

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 3-1-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 3-1-3, cybersecurity requirements for business continuity management must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-1-1 Establishing a disaster recovery center for critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-1-2 Incorporating critical systems within disaster recovery plans.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-1-3 Conducting periodical tests to ensure the efficiency of disaster recovery plans for critical systems, at least once annually.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-1-4 NCA recommends conducting periodical live Disaster Recovery (DR) test for critical systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the inclusion of the cybersecurity resiliency requirements within the organization’s business continuity management and to remediate and minimize the impacts on systems, information processing facilities and critical e-services from disasters caused by cybersecurity incidents.

controls.title: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 4-1, cybersecurity requirements for

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls in ECC subdomain 4-1, cybersecurity requirements for contracts and agreements with third-parties must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 4-1-1-1 Screening or vetting of outsourcing and managed services companies and personnel who work on critical systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 4-1-1-2 Outsourcing and managed services of critical systems must rely on Saudi companies and organizations, in accordance with the relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of assets against cybersecurity risks related to third parties, including outsourcing and managed services as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 4-2-3, cybersecurity requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 4-2-3, cybersecurity requirements related to the use of hosting and cloud computing services must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 4-2-1-1 Hosting of critical systems and any part of their technical components must be inside the organization or within cloud computing services provided by government organizations or Saudi companies that are in compliance with NCA’s Cloud Cybersecurity Controls (CCC), taking into account the classification of the hosted data.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the proper and efficient remediation of cyber risks and the implementation of cybersecurity requirements related to hosting and cloud computing as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations. It is also to ensure the protection of the organization’s information technology assets hosted on the cloud or processed/managed by third parties.

props.name: description
props.value: Cybersecurity controls for cloud service providers (CSP) and tenants (CST).

controls.title: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1, the Authorizing Official shall also identi

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1, the Authorizing Official shall also identify, document, and approve:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-1-P-1-1 Cybersecurity roles and RACI assignment for all stakeholders of the cloud services including the Authorizing Official's roles and responsibilities.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the roles and responsibilities are defined for all parties participating in implementing the cloud cybersecurity controls, including the roles and responsibilities of the head of the CSP and CST or his/her delegate, referred to in these controls as the “Authorizing Official.”

controls.title: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1, the Authorizing Official shall also identi

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the ECC control 1-4-1, the Authorizing Official shall also identify, document, and approve:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-1-T-1-1 Cybersecurity roles and RACI assignment for all stakeholders of the cloud services including the Authorizing Official's roles and responsibilities.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the roles and responsibilities are defined for all parties participating in implementing the cloud cybersecurity controls, including the roles and responsibilities of the head of the CSP and CST or his/her delegate, referred to in these controls as the “Authorizing Official.”

controls.title: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5, sh

props.name: conditional
props.value: if they are related to the CST

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5, shall also include for the CSP, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-P-1-1 Defining acceptable risk levels for the cloud services and clarifying them to the CST if they are related to the CST.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-P-1-2 Considering data and information classification in cybersecurity risk management methodology.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-P-1-3 Developing cybersecurity risk register for cloud services and monitoring it periodically according to the risks.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure managing cybersecurity risks in a methodological approach in order to protect the CSP’s and CST’s information and technology assets as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5 sha

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity risk management methodology mentioned in the ECC Subdomain 1-5 shall also include for the CST, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-T-1-1 Defining acceptable risk levels for the cloud services.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-T-1-2 Considering data and information classification accredited by CST in cybersecurity risk management methodology.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-T-1-3 Developing cybersecurity risk register for cloud services and monitoring it periodically according to the risks.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure managing cybersecurity risks in a methodological approach in order to protect the CSP’s and CST’s information and technology assets as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

groups.title: Compliance with Cybersecurity Standards, Laws, and Regulations

controls.title: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1, the CSP legislative and regulatory complia

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1, the CSP legislative and regulatory compliance should include as a minimum with the following requirements:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-P-1-1 Continuous compliance with all laws, regulations, instructions, decisions, regulatory frameworks and controls, and mandates regarding cybersecurity in the Kingdom

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the CSPs’ and CSTs’ cybersecurity program is in compliance with related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1, the CST legislative and regulatory complia

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the ECC control 1-7-1, the CST legislative and regulatory compliance should include as a minimum with the following requirements:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-T-1-1 Continuous or real-time compliance monitoring of the CSP with relevant cybersecurity legislation and contract clauses.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the CSPs’ and CSTs’ cybersecurity program is in compliance with related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC controls 1-9-3 and 1-9-4, the following re

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC controls 1-9-3 and 1-9-4

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC controls 1-9-3 and 1-9-4, the following requirements should be covered prior and during the professional relationship of personnel with the CSP as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-P-1-1 Positions of cybersecurity functions in CSP’s data centers within the Kingdom must be filled with qualified and suitable Saudi nationals.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-P-1-2 Screening or vetting candidates of personnel working inside the Kingdom who have access to Cloud Technology Stack, periodically.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-P-1-3 Cybersecurity policies as a prerequisite to access to Cloud Technology Stack, signed and appropriately approved.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements related to personnel (employees and contractors) are managed efficiently prior to employment, during employment and after termination/separation as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-5, the following requirements

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-5

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-5, the following requirements should be in place, as a minimum, for the termination/completion of a human resource’s professional relationship with the CSP:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-P-2-1 Assurance that assets owned by the entity (especially those with security exposure) are accounted for and returned upon termination.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements related to personnel (employees and contractors) are managed efficiently prior to employment, during employment and after termination/separation as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-3, the following requirements

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 1-9-3, the following requirements should be covered prior the professional relationship of staff with the CST shall cover, at a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-T-1-1 Screening or vetting candidates of personnel with access to Cloud Service sensitive functions (Key Management, Service Administration, Access Control).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements related to personnel (employees and contractors) are managed efficiently prior to employment, during employment and after termination/separation as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be identif

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be identified, documented, and approved.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in change management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets as per CSPs policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be applied

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be applied

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in change management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets as per CSPs policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: Cybersecurity for change management in the CSP shall cover, as a minimum:

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity for change management in the CSP shall cover, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-5-P-3-1 Processes and procedures to securely implement changes (planned works) in production systems, with priority given to cybersecurity observations.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-5-P-3-2 Process for the implementation of cybersecurity exceptional changes (e.g.: changes during incident restoration).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in change management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets as per CSPs policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be applied

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cybersecurity requirements for change management within the CSP shall be applied and reviewed periodically.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are included in change management methodology and procedures in order to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets as per CSPs policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1, the CSP shall cover the followin

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1, the CSP shall cover the following additional controls for cybersecurity requirements for cybersecurity event logs and monitoring management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-P-1-1 Inventory of all information and technology assets using suitable techniques such as Configuration Management Database (CMDB) or similar capability containing an inventory of all technical assets.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-P-1-2 Identifying assets owners and involving them in the asset management lifecycle.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the CSP and CST has an accurate and detailed inventory of information and technology assets in order to support the entity cybersecurity and operational requirements to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1, the CST shall cover the followin

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to controls in the ECC control 2-1, the CST shall cover the following additional controls for cybersecurity requirements for cybersecurity event logs and monitoring management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-T-1-1 Inventory of all cloud services and information and technology assets related to the cloud services.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the CSP and CST has an accurate and detailed inventory of information and technology assets in order to support the entity cybersecurity and operational requirements to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-1 Identity and access management of generic accounts credentials for accountability cannot be assigned for a specific individual.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-2 Secure session management, including session authenticity, session lockout, and session timeout termination.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-3 Multi-factor authentication for privileged users, and candidates of personnel with access to Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-4 Formal process to detect and prevent unauthorized access (e.g., unsuccessful login attempt threshold).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-5 Utilizing secure methods and algorithms for saving and processing passwords, such as: Secure Hashing functions.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-6 Secure management of third- party personnel’s accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-7 Access control enforced to management systems, administrative consoles.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-8 Masking of displayed authentication inputs, especially passwords, to prevent shoulder surfing.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-9 Getting CST’s approval before accessing any CST-related asset by the CSP or CSP’s third parties.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-10 Capability to immediately interrupt a remote access session and prevent any future access for a user.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-11 Provision to CSTs of multi-factor authentication services for privileged cloud users.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-P-1-12 Assurance of restricted and controlled access to storage systems and means (such as Storage Area Network (SAN)).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-2-3, the CST shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-T-1-1 Identity and access management for all cloud credentials along their full lifecycle.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-T-1-2 Confidentiality of cloud user identification, cloud credential and cloud access rights information, including the requirement on users to keep them private (for employed, third party and CST personnel).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-T-1-3 Secure session management, including session authenticity, session lockout, and session timeout termination on the cloud.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-T-1-4 Multi-factor authentication for privileged cloud users.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-T-1-5 Formal process to detect and prevent unauthorized access to cloud (such as a threshold of unsuccessful login attempts).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

groups.title: Information System and Information Processing Facilities Protection

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for information system and processing facilities protection requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-1 Ensuring that all configurations are applied in accordance with CSP’s cybersecurity standards.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-2 Assurance of separation and isolation of data, environments, and information systems across CSTs, to prevent data commingling.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-3 Adopting cybersecurity principles for technical system configurations adhering to the minimum functionality principle.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-4 Ability of the Cloud Technology Stacks to securely handle input validation, exceptions and failure.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-5 Full isolation of security functions and applications from other functions and applications in the Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-6 Notification to CSTs with cybersecurity requirements provided by the CSP that are useable by the CST.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-7 Detection and prevention of unauthorized changes to software programs, and systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-8 Complete isolation and protection of multiple guest environments.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-9 The community cloud services provided to CSTs (government entities and CNI entities) shall be isolated from any other cloud computing provided to entities outside the scope of work.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-P-1-10 Modern technologies, such as Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) technologies, to ensure that the information servers and devices of CSP’s information processing systems and devices of are ready for rapid response to incidents.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of information systems and information processing facilities (including workstations and infrastructures) against cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, the CST shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for information system and processing facilities protection requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-T-1-1 Verifying that the CSP isolates the community cloud services provided to CSTs (government entities and CNI entities) from any other cloud computing provided to entities outside the scope of work.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of information systems and information processing facilities (including workstations and infrastructures) against cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for networks security management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-1 Monitoring of traffic across the external and internal networks to detect anomalies.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-2 Network isolation and protection of Cloud Technology Stack network from other internal and external networks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-3 Protection from denial-of-service attacks (including Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS)).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-4 Protection of data transmitted through the network; from and to the Cloud Technology Stack network using cryptography primitives; for management and administrative access.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-5 Access control between different network segments.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-P-1-6 Isolation between cloud service delivery network, cloud management network and CSP enterprise network.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of CSP’s and CST’s network from cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, the CST shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for networks security management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-T-1-1 Protecting the connection channel with CSP.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of CSP’s and CST’s network from cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for mobile device security, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-P-1-1 Inventory of all end user and mobile devices.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-P-1-2 Centralized mobile device security management.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-P-1-3 Screen looking for end user devices.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-P-1-4 Data sanitation and secure disposal for end-user devices, especially for those with exposure to the Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of mobile devices (including laptops, smartphones, and tablets) from cyber risks and to ensure the secure handling of the CSPs’ and CSTs’ information (including sensitive information) while utilizing mobile devices.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-6-3, the CST shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for mobile device security, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-T-1-1 Data sanitation and secure disposal for end-user devices with access to the cloud services.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of mobile devices (including laptops, smartphones, and tablets) from cyber risks and to ensure the secure handling of the CSPs’ and CSTs’ information (including sensitive information) while utilizing mobile devices.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for data and information protection requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-P-1-1 Prohibiting the use of Cloud Technology Stack’s data in any environment other than production environment, except after applying strict controls for protecting that data, such as data masking or data scrambling techniques.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-P-1-2 Provision to CSTs of securely data storage processes, procedures, and technologies to comply with related legal and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-P-1-3 Disposal of CST’s data should be performed in a secure manner on termination or expiry of the contract with the CSP.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-P-1-4 Commitment to maintain the confidentiality of the CST’s data and information, according to related legal and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-P-1-5 Providing CSTs with secure means to export and transfer data and virtual infrastructure.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of CSPs’ and CSTs’ data and information asper entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, the CST shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for protecting CST’s data and information in cloud computing, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-T-1-1 Exit Strategy to ensure means for secure disposal of data on termination or expiry of the contract with the CSP.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-T-1-2 Using secure means to export and transfer data and virtual infrastructure.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of CSPs’ and CSTs’ data and information asper entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to Subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional Subcontrols for cryptography, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-P-1-1 Technical mechanisms and cryptographic primitives for strong encryption, in according to the advanced level in the National Cryptographic Standards (NCS-1:2020).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-P-1-2 Certification authority and issuance capability in a secure manner, or usage of certificates from a trusted certification authority.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the proper and efficient use of cryptography to protect information assets as per policies, procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3, the CST shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-8-3, the CST shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cryptography, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-T-1-1 Technical mechanisms and cryptographic primitives for strong encryption, in according to the advanced level in the National Cryptographic Standards (NCS-1:2020).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-T-1-2 Encryption of data and information transferred to or transferred out of the cloud according to the relevant law and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the proper and efficient use of cryptography to protect information assets as per policies, procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-9-3, the CSP shall cover the fol

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-9-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-9-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for backup and recovery management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-P-1-1 Securing access, storage and transfer of CST’s data backups and its mediums, and protecting it against damage, amendment, or unauthorized access.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-P-1-2 Securing access, storage and transfer of Cloud Technology Stack backups and its mediums, and protecting it against damage, amendment, or unauthorized access.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of CSPs’ data and information including information systems andsoftware configurations from cyber risks as per entity-related policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, the CSP shall cover the fo

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for vulnerability management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-P-1-1 Assessing and remediating vulnerabilities on external components of Cloud Technology Stack at least once every month, and at least once every three months for internal components of Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-P-1-2 Notification to CSTs of identified vulnerabilities that may affecting them, and safeguards in place.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely detection and effective remediation of technical vulnerabilities to prevent or minimize the probability of exploiting these vulnerabilities to launch cyber attacks against the CSP and CST.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, the CST shall cover the fo

props.name: frequency
props.value: at least once every three months

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, the CST shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for vulnerability management requirements, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-T-1-1 Assessing and remediating vulnerabilities cloud services and at least once every three months.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-T-1-2 Management of CSP-notified vulnerabilities safeguards in place.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely detection and effective remediation of technical vulnerabilities to prevent or minimize the probability of exploiting these vulnerabilities to launch cyber attacks against the CSP and CST.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-11-3, the CSP shall cover the fo

props.name: frequency
props.value: at least once every six months

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-11-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-11-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for penetration testing, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-10-P-1-1 Scope of penetration tests must cover Cloud Technology Stack and must be conducted at least once every six months.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To assess and evaluate the efficiency of the CSP’s cybersecurity defense capabilities through simulated cyber-attacks to discover unknown weaknesses within the technical infrastructure that may lead to a cyber breach.

groups.title: Cybersecurity Event Logs and Monitoring Management

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, the CSP shall cover the fo

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for cybersecurity event logs and monitoring management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-1 Activating and protecting event logs and audit trails of Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-2 Activating and collecting login attempts history.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-3 Activating and protecting all event logs of activities and operations performed by the CSP at the tenant level in order to support forensic analysis.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-4 Protecting cybersecurity event logs from alteration, disclosure, destruction and unauthorized access and unauthorized release, in accordance with regulatory, or law requirements.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-5 Continuous cybersecurity events monitoring using SIEM technique covering the full Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-6 Reviewing cybersecurity event logs and audit trails periodically, covering CSP events in the Cloud Technology Stack.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-7 Automated monitoring and logging of remote access sessions event logs.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-P-1-8 Secure handling of user-related data found in the audit trails and the cybersecurity event logs.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Ensure timely collection, analysis, and monitoring of cybersecurity event logs for the proactive detection and effective management of cyber-attacks to prevent or minimize the impact on the CSPs’ and CSTs’ business.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, the CST shall cover the fo

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, the CST shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for cybersecurity event logs and monitoring management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-T-1-1 Activating and collecting of login event logs, and cybersecurity event logs on assets related to cloud services.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-T-1-2 Monitoring shall include all activated cybersecurity logs on the cloud services of the CST.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Ensure timely collection, analysis, and monitoring of cybersecurity event logs for the proactive detection and effective management of cyber-attacks to prevent or minimize the impact on the CSPs’ and CSTs’ business.

controls.title: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-13-3, the CSP shall cover the fo

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-13-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to subcontrols in the ECC control 2-13-3, the CSP shall cover the following additional subcontrols for cybersecurity requirements for cybersecurity incident and threat management, as a minimum:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-P-1-1 Subscribing in authorized and specialized entities and groups to stay up-to-date on Cybersecurity threats, common practices, and key know-how.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-P-1-2 Training for employees and third-party personnel to respond to cybersecurity incidents, in line with their roles and responsibilities.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-P-1-3 Periodically testing the incident response capability.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-P-1-4 Root Cause Analysis of cybersecurity incidents and developing plans to address them.

parts.name: item
