parts.prose: 3-1-2-2 Requiring contractual commitment by consultancy services including employees non-disclosure agreements and secure disposal the organization’s data at the end of the contract or in case of contract termination, including providing evidences of such disposal to the organization.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-3 Documenting all data sharing operations within consultancy services, including data sharing justification.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-4 Requiring consultancy services to notify the organization immediately in case of cybersecurity incident that may affect data that has been shared or created.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-5 Reclassifying data to the least level to achieve the objective before sharing it with consultancy services using data masking or data scrambling techniques.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-6 Dedicating a closed room for the consultancy services employees to perform their work, in addition to providing dedicated organization owned devices to share and process data.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-7 Activating access control system to allow only authorized access to the closed room.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-8 Preventing carrying out of devices, storage media and documents outside the closed room, as well as the entry of any other electronic devices.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of assets against the cybersecurity risks related to third parties including outsourcing, managed services and consultancy services as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

props.name: description
props.value: Cybersecurity controls for telework and remote working scenarios.

controls.title: Referring to control 1-3-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity policies and procedures mus

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Referring to control 1-3-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity policies and procedures must include the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-1-1-1 Defining and documenting the telework cybersecurity requirements and controls as part of the organization’s cybersecurity policies.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are documented, communicated and complied with by the organization as per related laws and regulations, and organizational requirements.

controls.title: In addition to the controls within subdomain 1-5 in the ECC, requirements for cy

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls within subdomain 1-5 in the ECC, requirements for cybersecurity risk management should include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-1-1 Assessment of the cybersecurity risks for telework systems, once per year at least.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-1-2 Assessment of cybersecurity risks during planning and before permitting telework for any service or system.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-2-1-3 Including the cybersecurity risks related to telework systems and its related services and systems in the entity's cybersecurity risk register, and monitoring it at least once a year.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure managing cybersecurity risks in a methodological approach in order to protect the organization’s information and technology assets as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 1-10-3 in the ECC, the cybersecur

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 1-10-3 in the ECC, the cybersecurity awareness program must cover the awareness about the potential cyber risks and threats related to telework, including the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-1 Secure use of telework devices and how to protect them.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-2 Secure handling of identities and passwords.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-3 Protection of the stored data on the telework devices, and to be handled based on its classification.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-4 Secure handling of applications and solutions used for telework such as: virtual conferencing and collaboration, and file sharing solutions.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-5 Secure handling of home networks, making sure it is configured in a secure way.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-6 Avoidance of teleworking using unreliable public devices or networks or while in public places.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-7 Unauthorized physical access, loss, theft, and sabotage of technical assets and telework systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-8 To Communicate directly with the cybersecurity department If a cybersecurity threat is suspected.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that personnel are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities and have the essential cybersecurity awareness. It is also to ensure that personnel are provided with the required cybersecurity training, skills and credentials needed to accomplish their cybersecurity responsibilities and to protect the organization’s information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 1-10-4 in the ECC, employees must

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 1-10-4 in the ECC, employees must be trained with the required technical skills to ensure the implementation of the cybersecurity requirements when handling telework systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that personnel are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities and have the essential cybersecurity awareness. It is also to ensure that personnel are provided with the required cybersecurity training, skills and credentials needed to accomplish their cybersecurity responsibilities and to protect the organization’s information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the controls within subdomain 2-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity requi

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls within subdomain 2-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for asset management related to telework systems should include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-1-1 Identifying and maintaining an annually-updated Inventory of information and technology assets of the telework systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the organization has an accurate and detailed inventory of information and technology assets in order to support the organization’s cybersecurity and operational requirements to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-2-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity r

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-2-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-1 Managing telework access rights based on need, considering the sensitivity of the systems, the level of access rights and the type of devices used by employees for telework.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-2 Restricting remote access for the same user from multiple computers at the same time (Concurrent Logins).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-3 Using secure standards to manage identities and passwords used in the telework systems.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

controls.title: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user’s identities and access right

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user’s identities and access rights used for telework must be reviewed at least once every year.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

groups.title: Information System and Processing Facilities Protection

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, cybersecurity requireme

props.name: frequency
props.value: at least once every three months

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-3-3, cybersecurity requirements for protecting telework systems and information processing facilities must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-1 Applying updates and security patches for telework systems at least once every three months.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-2 Reviewing telework systems’ configurations and hardening at least once every year.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-3 Reviewing and changing default configurations, and ensuring the removal of hard-coded, backdoor and/or default passwords.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-4 Securing Session Management which includes the session authenticity, lockout, and timeout.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-3-1-5 Restricting the activation of the features and services of the telework systems based on needs, provided that potential cyber risks are analyzed in case there is a need to activate them.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of information systems and information processing facilities (including workstations and infrastructures) against cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, cybersecurity requireme

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-5-3, cybersecurity requirements of telework systems’ network security management must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-1 Restrictions on network services, protocols and ports used to access remotely, specifically to internal systems and to only be opened based on need.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-2 Reviewing firewall rules and configurations, at least once every year.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-3 Protecting against Distributed Denial of Service Attack (DDoS) attacks to limit risks arising from these attacks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-4-1-4 Protecting against Advanced Persistent Threats (APT) at the network layer.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of organization’s network from cyber risks.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-6-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity r

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-6-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for mobile device security related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-1-1 Central management of mobile devices and BYODs using a Mobile Device Management system (MDM).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-5-1-2 Applying updates and security patches on mobile devices, at least once every month.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of mobile devices (including laptops, smartphones, tablets) from cyber risks and to ensure the secure handling of the organization’s information (including sensitive information) while utilizing Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policy.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, cybersecurity requireme

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-7-3, cybersecurity requirements for protecting and handling data and information must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-1 Identifying classified data, according to the relevant regulations, that can be used, accessed or dealt with through telework systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-2 Protecting classified data, which was identified in control 2-6-1-1, using controls such as: not allowing the use of a specific type of classified data, or by the use of technology (e.g. Data leakage Prevention), such controls and technologies can be determined by analyzing the cyber risks of the organization.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of organization’s data and information as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-8-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity r

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-8-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for cryptography related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-1-1 The use of updated and secure methods and algorithms for encryption over the entire network connection used for telework, according to the Advanced level within the National Cryptography Standards (NCS 1:2020).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-8-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for cryptography related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-9-3, cybersecurity requireme

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-9-3, cybersecurity requirements for backup and recovery management must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-8-1-1 Performing backup within planned intervals, according to the organization’s risk assessment.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of organization’s data and information including information systems and software configurations from cyber risks as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-9-3-3, a periodical test must be conducte

props.name: frequency
props.value: at least once every six months

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-9-3-3, a periodical test must be conducted at least once every six months in order to determine the efficiency of recovering telework systems backups.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of organization’s data and information including information systems and software configurations from cyber risks as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, cybersecurity requirem

props.name: frequency
props.value: at least once every three months

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-10-3, cybersecurity requirements for technical vulnerabilities management of telework systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-1-1 Assessing vulnerabilities on technical components of telework systems, and to be classified based on criticality at least once every three months.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-9-1-2 Remediating vulnerabilities for telework systems, at least once every three months.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely detection and effective remediation of technical vulnerabilities to prevent or minimize the probability of exploiting these vulnerabilities to launch cyber attacks against the organization.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-11-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-11-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for penetration testing related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-10-1-1 Scope of penetration tests must cover all of the telework systems’ technical components.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To assess and evaluate the efficiency of the organization’s cybersecurity defense capabilities through simulated cyber-attacks to discover unknown weaknesses within the technical infrastructure that may lead to a cyber breach.

controls.title: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-11-3-2, penetration tests must be conduct

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-11-3-2, penetration tests must be conducted on telework systems at least once every year.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To assess and evaluate the efficiency of the organization’s cybersecurity defense capabilities through simulated cyber-attacks to discover unknown weaknesses within the technical infrastructure that may lead to a cyber breach.

groups.title: Cybersecurity Events Logs and Monitoring Management

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirem

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirements for event logs and monitoring management for telework systems must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-1 Activating cybersecurity events logs on all technical components of telework systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-2 Monitoring and analyzing user behavior (UBA).

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-3 Monitoring telework systems events around the clock.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-11-1-4 Updating and implementing cybersecurity monitoring procedures around the clock, to include monitoring remote access operations, especially remote access from outside the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, after checking their authenticity.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely collection, analysis and monitoring of cybersecurity events for early detection of potential cyber-attacks in order to prevent or minimize the negative impacts on the organization’s operations.

controls.title: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-12-3-5, retention period of cybersecurity

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With reference to the ECC subcontrol 2-12-3-5, retention period of cybersecurity’s telework systems event logs must be 12 months minimum, in accordance with relevant legislative and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely collection, analysis and monitoring of cybersecurity events for early detection of potential cyber-attacks in order to prevent or minimize the negative impacts on the organization’s operations.

controls.title: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-13-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the sub-controls within control 2-13-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for incident and threat management related to telework systems shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-1-1 Updating cyber security incidents response plans and contact information within the organization in a way that is compatible with the telework situation and to ensure the ability to communicate and the preparedness of the incident response teams.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-1-2 Periodically obtaining and dealing with threat intelligence information related to telework systems.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-12-1-3 Addressing and implementing the recommendations and alerts for cyber security incidents and threats issued by the Sector regulator or by the National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely identification, detection, effective management and handling of cybersecurity incidents and threats to prevent or minimize negative impacts on organization’s operation taking into consideration the Royal Decree number 37140, dated 14/8/1438H.

groups.title: Third Party And Cloud Computing Cybersecurity

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 4-2-3, cybersecurity requireme

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in the ECC control 4-2-3, cybersecurity requirements related to the use of hosting and cloud computing services must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-1-1 The location of the hosted telework systems must be inside the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the proper and efficient remediation of cyber risks and the implementation of cybersecurity requirements related to hosting and cloud computing as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations. It is also to ensure the protection of the organization’s information and technology assets hosted on the cloud or processed/managed by third-parties.

groups.title: Organisations' Social Media Accounts Cybersecurity Controls (OSMACC-1:2021)

props.name: description
props.value: Cybersecurity controls for organisational social media account management.

controls.title: Referring to control 1-3-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity policies and procedures mus

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-1-1-1 Defining and documenting the cybersecurity requirements for organizations’ social media accounts as part of the organization’s cybersecurity policies.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity requirements are documented, communicated and complied with by the organization as per related laws and regulations, and organizational requirements.

controls.title: In addition to the controls within subdomain 1-5 in the ECC, requirements for cy

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the controls within subdomain 1-5 in the ECC

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-9-4 in the ECC, the cybersecurit

props.value: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-9-4 in the ECC

parts.prose: 1-3-1-1 Cybersecurity awareness about social media accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-3-1-2 Implementation of and compliance with the cybersecurity requirements as per the organizational cybersecurity policies and procedures for the organization’s social media accounts.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that cybersecurity risks and requirements related to personnel (employees and contractors) are managed efficiently prior to employment, during employment and after termination/separation as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-3 in the ECC, the cybersecuri

props.name: conditional
props.value: if a cybersecurity threat is suspected

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-3 in the ECC

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-3 in the ECC, the cybersecurity awareness program must cover the awareness about the potential cyber risks and threats related to the organization’s social media accounts and the secure use to minimize these risks and threats, including the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-1 Secure use and protection of devices dedicated to the organization’s social media accounts and ensuring that they do not contain classified data or used for personal purposes.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-2 Secure handling of identities, passwords and security questions.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-3 Organization’s social media accounts restoration plan and dealing with cybersecurity incidents.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-4 Secure handling of applications and solutions used for the organization’s social media accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-5 Not to use the organization’s social media accounts for personal purposes such as browsing.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-6 Avoiding accessing the organization’s social media accounts using untrusted public devices or networks.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 1-4-1-7 Communicating directly with the cybersecurity department if a cybersecurity threat is suspected.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that personnel are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities and have the essential cybersecurity awareness. It is also to ensure that personnel are provided with the required cybersecurity training, skills and credentials needed to accomplish their cybersecurity responsibilities and to protect the organization’s information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-4 in the ECC, personnel respo

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-4 in the ECC

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols within control 1-10-4 in the ECC, personnel responsible for managing the organization’s social media accounts must be trained on the required technical skills, plans and procedures necessary to ensure the implementation of the cybersecurity requirements and practices when using the organization’s social media accounts.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that personnel are aware of their cybersecurity responsibilities and have the essential cybersecurity awareness. It is also to ensure that personnel are provided with the required cybersecurity training, skills and credentials needed to accomplish their cybersecurity responsibilities and to protect the organization’s information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the controls within subdomain 2-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity requi

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the controls within subdomain 2-1 in the ECC

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the controls within subdomain 2-1 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for managing infromation and technology assets must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-1-1-1 Identifying and inventorying organization’s social media accounts, and information and technology assets related to them, and updating them at least once, every year.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure that the organization has an accurate and detailed inventory of information and technology assets in order to support the organization’s cybersecurity and operational requirements to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information and technology assets.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 2-2-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity re

props.name: ecc-reference

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols within control 2-2-3 in the ECC, cybersecurity requirements for identity and access management related to organization’s social media accounts shall include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-1 Using social media accounts designated for organizations, not individuals.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-2 Registering using official information (official specific social media email and official mobile number), and do not use personal information.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-3 Verifying organization’s social media accounts whenever possible and maintaining a consistent identity across all organization’s social media accounts used; to facilitate knowledge of official accounts, and to discover fraud or unofficial accounts.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-4 Using a secure and specific password for each organization’s social media account, changing the password regularly, and not to repeate use of passwords.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-5 Using multi-factor authentication for organization’s social media accounts logins.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-6 Activating and updating security questions and documenting them in a safe place.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-7 Managing organization’s social media accounts access rights based on business need, considering the sensitivity of the accounts, the level of access rights and the type of devices and systems used.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-8 Restricting access rights of service providers of social media management, social media monitoring or brand protection.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-2-1-9 Restricting access to organization’s social media accounts to specific devices.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

controls.title: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user identities and access rights used

parts.prose: With reference to ECC subcontrol 2-2-3-5, user identities and access rights used for organization’s social media accounts must be reviewed at least once every year.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the secure and restricted logical access to information and technology assets in order to prevent unauthorized access and allow only authorized access for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

parts.prose: 2-5-1-1 Technology assets for management of organization’s social media accounts must not contain classified data, per relevant regulations.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of organization’s data and information as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

groups.title: Cybersecurity Events Logs and Monitoring Management

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirements

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-12-3

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols in ECC control 2-12-3, cybersecurity requirements for event logs and monitoring management for organization’s social media accounts and technology assets related to them must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-1 Activating all notifications and cybersecurity alerts for organization’s social media accounts and cybersecurity events logs on related technology assets.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-2 Following organization’s social media accounts and monitoring them to ensure that they do not post any unauthorized content, or login any unauthorized access.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-3 Monitoring social media networks to ensure the organization is not being impersonated.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-6-1-4 Automated monitoring for any change in the accounts pattern, indicators of compromise, or the publication of any unauthorized content or impersonation of the organization.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely collection, analysis and monitoring of cybersecurity events for early detection of potential cyber-attacks in order to prevent or minimize the negative impacts on the organization’s operations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 2-13-3 in ECC, cybersecurity requi

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the subcontrols within control 2-13-3 in ECC

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols within control 2-13-3 in ECC, cybersecurity requirements for incident and threat management in the organization must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 2-7-1-1 Developing a plan to recover the organization’s social media accounts and to deal with cyber incidents.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure timely identification, detection, effective management and handling of cybersecurity incidents and threats to prevent or minimize negative impacts on organization’s operation taking into consideration the Royal Decree number 37140, dated 14/8/1438H.

groups.title: Third Party and Cloud Computing Cybersecurity

controls.title: A need assessment for the use of social media management, automated monitoring o

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A need assessment for the use of social media management, automated monitoring or brand protection services along with associcated cybersecurity risks must be conducted.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of assets against the cybersecurity risks related to third-parties including outsourcing and managed services as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

controls.title: In addition to the subcontrols within control 4-1-2 in ECC, cybersecurity requir

props.name: ecc-reference
props.value: In addition to the subcontrols within control 4-1-2 in ECC

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In addition to the subcontrols within control 4-1-2 in ECC, cybersecurity requirements for use of social media management, automated monitoring or brand protection services in the organization must include at least the following:

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-1 Non-disclosure clauses and secure removal of organization’s data by the third-party upon service termination.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-2 Communication procedures to report vulnerabilities and cyber incidents.

parts.name: item
parts.prose: 3-1-2-3 Requirments for the third-party to comply with cybersecurity requirements and policies to protect organizations’ social media accounts, and related laws and regulation.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: To ensure the protection of assets against the cybersecurity risks related to third-parties including outsourcing and managed services as per organizational policies and procedures, and related laws and regulations.

parts.name: objective

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Root certificates must be distributed to relying parties and signatories.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] A trusted CA must be used.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Distribution and Installation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Keys must be distributed to their users securely and must be under the users control.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Distribution and Installation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Keys must be transported securely by protecting their confidentiality and authenticity.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Distribution and Installation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] All copies of keys must be securely installed and stored.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Distribution and Installation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Public keys must be transported and authenticity (but not privacy) must be protected by using certificates.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Distribution and Installation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Private keys must be protected and authorized by the owner/ third party or CA.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Use

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Keys must be protected against unauthorized use during their lifetimes.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Use

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Keys must be protected against misuse from the owners, with key storage on secure hardware and/or secure software (authorization checks).

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Storage

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Organizations must require secure backups of keys (for internal or law enforcement use) when encryption is supported.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Storage

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Keys used for non-repudiation must be under the sole control of the user.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Revocation/Validation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] In distributed systems, special measures such as updated versions of the Certificate Revocation List (CRL) and the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) must be implemented to avoid relying on keys that have expired.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Revocation/Validation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Key validation must be done by checking the CRL or OCSP servers.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Archive

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] The archival process must be secured, and confidentiality must be ensured to preserve the secrecy of information encrypted with archived keys.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Archive

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Expired keys must be archived to keep old data accessible when encryption is supported.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Archive

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Archival systems must follow the retention periods as per relevant regulations.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

controls.title: Key Destruction

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] When key lifetime expires and there is no need for it to be archived or stored, it must be removed from hardware via a secure deletion process.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Use of asymmetric keys must be monitored.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: KLM Processes

parts.name: objective

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Pseudo Random Number Generation (PRNG)

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Pseudo Random Number Generation (PRNG)

controls.title: Cryptographic Operations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Cryptographic operations must be performed in certified hardware components, e.g. smart cards to protect the secret and/or private keys.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Side-Channel Attacks

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: to minimize the risk of side-channel attacks

controls.title: Side-Channel Analysis

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Analyse thoroughly the effects of such side channels on certified hardware components in a specialized laboratory during the development process.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Side-Channel Attacks

controls.title: Ciphertext Protection

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: [Mandatory] Protect ciphertexts using MAC and verify ciphertexts before performing any other cryptographic operations.

parts.name: objective
parts.prose: Side-Channel Attacks
